---
title: "Functional clustering of epidemic curves for multi-disease host resistance profiling: a case study for three foliar wheat diseases"
resources:
  - video_trigo.mp4
---

```{=html}
<div class="hero-video-banner column-screen">
  <video src="video_trigo.mp4" autoplay loop muted playsinline></video>
  <video src="video_trigo.mp4" autoplay loop muted playsinline></video>
  <video src="video_trigo.mp4" autoplay loop muted playsinline></video>
  <video src="video_trigo.mp4" autoplay loop muted playsinline></video>
  <video src="video_trigo.mp4" autoplay loop muted playsinline></video>
</div>
```

# Data analysis

Welcome to the companion website for the analysis code associated with the De Cól et al. (2026) paper. This repository contains the full R scripts and analytical pipelines used to characterize and classify resistance phenotypes of wheat cultivars to multiple foliar diseases.

## Overview

This website provides the analysis code associated with the De Cól et al. study on functional phenotyping of wheat resistance to multiple foliar diseases. The repository contains the R scripts and analytical pipelines used to model disease progress curves, classify resistance phenotypes, identify informative disease assessment stages, and evaluate multi-disease resistance in wheat cultivars.

The study focused on three important foliar fungal diseases of wheat: leaf rust, powdery mildew, and tan spot. Using multi-environment field data collected across municipalities in Paraná and São Paulo, Brazil, from 2019 to 2025, disease progress curves were modeled along the Zadoks growth scale with hierarchical beta-regression generalized additive models. Functional distances among cultivar disease trajectories were then used to group cultivars according to similarity in epidemic development.

The code available here reproduces the main analytical steps of the study, including model fitting, functional distance estimation, hierarchical clustering, comparison with final-severity-based classifications, extraction of epidemic descriptors such as disease onset and maximum epidemic velocity, identification of optimal assessment stages, and multi-disease resistance analysis using an index-based approach and Pareto front analysis.

Overall, this repository supports reproducibility of the analyses and provides a workflow for using disease progress curves to characterize resistance phenotypes and identify wheat cultivars with resistance to multiple foliar diseases.

Please navigate using the **Analysis** dropdown menu in the top bar to explore the detailed code for each specific disease.

## Authors
- Monalisa Cristina De Col (1,2), Edson Giovanni Kochinski(3), Ronaldo S. Ortiz(3), Helio Antonio Wood Joris(3), Erick De Wolf (1), Emerson Medeiros Del Ponte(2)
(1) Department of Plant Pathology, Kansas State University, Manhattan, Kansas; (2) Departamento de Fitopatologia, Universidade Federal de Viçosa, Viçosa, Brazil; (3) Fundação ABC, Castro, Paraná, Brazil
